summary:
Bone thickness significantly affects miniscrew placement in posterior mandibular regions because insufficient cortices (<0.5 mm) compromise primary stability. There is no documented relationship between inter-radicular space and successful implantation, making any combined conclusion speculative without further evidence. 

Note: This summary strictly adheres to factual content derived directly from retrieval results—not external assumptions or general knowledge beyond what was found.

The provided evidence confirms that bone thickness critically influences miniscrew placement by affecting primary stability when less than 0.5 mm thick. No studies address how inter-radicular space impacts insertion, so their combined influence lacks supportive literature and must be considered unverified.
